listing.title: Studio near UBC, $1650, utilities included
listing.description: Private studio, quiet building, normal lease. Viewing available evenings. No upfront payment before viewing. Application fee: none.

signals.label: BC: Mentions an application or processing fee
signals.why_it_matters: In BC, application or processing fees are a common predatory pattern and may violate standard tenancy expectations.

signals.label: Mentions an application fee
signals.why_it_matters: Extra fees may indicate predatory terms or policy violations depending on the province.

topReasons: BC: Mentions an application or processing fee
Photo looks reused from other listings
Photo looks reused from other listings
recommendedActions: Ask for a written breakdown of all fees (rent, deposits, utilities) and confirm what is legally allowed in your province.
Request a standard lease agreement and read all clauses (fees, pet terms, penalties).
Compare against similar listings in the same area before committing.
redundancySteps: Do an in-person viewing (or live video tour) before sending any money.
Ask for the landlord/agent’s full name and verify ownership (land registry / property manager website).
Reverse-image search 2–3 listing photos to spot duplicates across cities.
Compare rent vs nearby similar units (same beds/baths) to detect bait pricing.
Never pay via e-Transfer, crypto, gift cards, or cash before the lease is signed and keys are verified.
If you have a lease draft, run it through the Lease Simplifier to flag predatory clauses.
